note: Snapshot is the prod ethflow (0xba3c..eadec) verified ABI; the v1 contract (0x40a5..2187) shares the createOrder interface (same selector 0x322bba21, same EthFlowOrder.Data; confirmed by identical Dune decode + identical raw-calldata selector), so both addresses are covered by the one create-order manifest. I1 completeness runs once against the prod snapshot's 6 external-mutating fns.

0x322bba21.name: createOrder
0x322bba21.reason: user native-ETH order placement (payable; msg.value = sellAmount + feeAmount wrapped to WETH). EthFlowOrder.Data carries the full order terms -> amm sign_intent_order (venue=cow_swap, OnchainTx). The dominant ethflow user action (9.3% of all settled CoW orders, L2 SCOPE ORACLE).

0x7bc41b96.name: invalidateOrder
0x7bc41b96.reason: DEFER: ethflow-level cancellation of a single ETH order; takes the full EthFlowOrder.Data tuple (not a uid). Mapping to cancel_intent_order needs the orderHash = keccak(order||owner||validTo), which the static V3 emit cannot compute (no $fn for arbitrary-struct hashing). Lower-frequency than placement; the dominant on-chain cancel path GPv2Settlement.invalidateOrder(orderUid) is already covered.

0x4cb76498.name: invalidateOrdersIgnoringNotAllowed
0x4cb76498.reason: DEFER: batch variant of the ethflow cancel (EthFlowOrder.Data[]); same orderHash-derivation limitation as invalidateOrder, and a batch-cancel maintenance action rather than an order intent.

0xde0e9a3e.name: unwrap
0xde0e9a3e.reason: permissionless WETH->ETH maintenance on the ethflow contract's OWN balance (used around settlement); not a user order/intent action — no swap terms to scope.

0xea598cb0.name: wrap
0xea598cb0.reason: permissionless ETH->WETH maintenance on the ethflow contract's own balance; not a user order/intent action.

0x4c84c1c8.name: wrapAll
0x4c84c1c8.reason: permissionless wrap of the ethflow contract's entire ETH balance to WETH; contract maintenance, not a user order/intent action.
